explanation: The natural language requirement is: 'PSV Mode: The breath cycle shall start with the inspiration phase.' The atomic proposition 'PSVMode' indicates that the device is in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode, and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' marks the start of the inspiration phase. Thus, the behavior to enforce is that when the PSVMode is active, the start of the breathing cycle ('breathingCycleStart') should coincide with (or directly lead to) the start of the inspiratory phase ('inspiratoryPhaseStart'). The best LTL template for this is 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', which can be instantiated as: G( (PSVMode & breathingCycleStart) -> inspiratoryPhaseStart ). This formula expresses that globally, whenever a breath cycle starts AND it is in PSV mode, the inspiration phase must start as well, matching the requirement that every breath in PSV mode must begin with inspiration.

The requirement states that when the system is in PSV Mode, the breath cycle must start with the inspiration phase. This translates to: if PSVMode is true (i.e., 'Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSV)' is active), the breathing cycle starts with the inspiration phase. This implies that at the beginning of a breathing cycle in PSVMode, the inspiratoryPhase should be active. Since 'breathingCycleStart' indicates the start of a breathing cycle, and 'inspiratoryPhase' indicates inspiration is active, we can formalize that whenever a breath cycle is initiated in PSVMode, the next state should be an active inspiratory phase. Thus, we use a template where whenever PSVMode and breathingCycleStart are true, then in the next state, inspiratoryPhase will be true.

The natural language states that in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode, each breath cycle must begin with the inspiration phase. The most direct atomic propositions related to this are PSVMode (the ventilation mode is PSV), and inspiratoryPhaseStart (start of inspiratory phase). Since we want to express that whenever PSVMode is active, the breath cycle must begin with inspiration, we can say: 

'Whenever PSVMode is selected/active, a breathing cycle starts with the start of the inspiration phase.' As there is no single atomic proposition for 'breathing cycle start,' and the requirement is that the breath cycle (in this mode) highlights inspiration as the start, the closest mapping is: Whenever PSVMode is active, breathingCycleStart must trigger inspiratoryPhaseStart. So, whenever a new breath cycle starts in PSVMode, an inspiration phase must be started. This can be captured by asserting that, globally, if a PSV mode is selected and a breath cycle starts, then the inspiratory phase should start as well. The most direct fit with the available templates is 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)' (Template 40), where bool_exp1: PSVMode, bool_exp2: breathingCycleStart, bool_exp3: inspiratoryPhaseStart. N_DURATION is not needed.

This template asserts: Globally, whenever we are in PSV mode and a new breathing cycle is starting, the inspiration phase must also start. This captures the property that every breath in PSV mode must begin with inspiration.

The natural language states that when the system is in PSV Mode, the breath cycle shall start with the inspiration phase. This is best modeled by asserting that, globally (at all points in time), when PSVMode is true (i.e., the ventilator is set to Pressure Support Ventilation Mode), the next event (immediately) is inspiratoryPhaseStart (i.e., the start of inspiratory phase). Thus, we use the template: 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))', where bool_exp2 is 'PSVMode' (the PSV mode is active), and bool_exp3 is 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' (the start of the inspiratory phase). This ensures that as soon as PSVMode is enabled, the very next thing that happens is the start of the inspiratory phase.

The requirement states that when Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode is active, each breath cycle should begin with the inspiration phase. This means that whenever PSVMode becomes active, the first phase in the breathing cycle should be the inspiration phase (indicated by 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'). 

The most compact and direct way to capture this is to require that whenever PSVMode holds, the start of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart) also holds. This matches the meaning that in PSV mode, the inspiration phase must be the start of the breath cycle. Therefore, we use the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', where 'bool_exp1' is 'PSVMode' and 'bool_exp3' is 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'.

This LTL guarantees that while in PSVMode, the inspiration phase always starts the breath cycle, as required.

The requirement states that, in PSV mode, each breath cycle must start with the inspiration phase. Translating this into LTL, whenever PSVMode is active (which can be indicated by PSVMode being true), then at the start of each breathing cycle, the inspiration phase must be started. Typically, a 'breathingCycleStart' marks the start of the breath cycle, and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' indicates the inspiration phase begins. This means: whenever PSVMode and a new breathing cycle (breathingCycleStart) occur, the start of the inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart) should immediately occur. This is best captured by stating: Globally, whenever PSVMode and breathingCycleStart is true, then inspiratoryPhaseStart is true in the next state. This is best expressed using template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), substituting 'PSVMode' for bool_exp1, 'breathingCycleStart' for bool_exp2, and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' for bool_exp3.

The requirement states that when in PSV mode, the breath cycle must start with the inspiration phase. The atomic proposition 'PSVMode' indicates that PSV mode is active, and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' indicates the start of the inspiration phase. Thus, the appropriate LTL expression is that whenever PSV mode becomes active ('PSVMode'), it must be followed immediately by the start of inspiratory phase ('inspiratoryPhaseStart'). This is expressed using the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> (X bool_exp3))', which means: 'Globally, whenever PSVMode holds, at the next step inspiratoryPhaseStart must hold.' N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The property expresses that whenever PSV mode is active (represented by 'PSVMode'), the breath cycle must begin with the inspiration phase. This is interpreted as: if PSV mode is active, the system should start the breathing cycle by activating the inspiratory phase, here defined as the start event 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'. This matches the requirement that the breath cycle in PSV Mode should always initiate with inspiration. Using the template 'globally, if in PSV mode then the breath cycle starts with the inspiration phase,' captures this intent with 'PSVMode' triggering 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' as the first phase of each breath cycle.

The requirement states that when the PSV mode is active, a breath cycle should start with the inspiratory phase. This means that in PSV mode, the system should begin the breathing cycle by entering the inspiratory phase. The relevant atomic propositions are 'PSVMode' (indicates PSV mode active), 'breathingCycleStart' (indicates the start of breath cycle), and 'inspiratoryPhase' (inspiratory phase active). The formula chosen requires that whenever both PSV mode is active and a breath cycle starts, the inspiratory phase must be active at that moment. Thus, the implication (PSVMode & breathingCycleStart) -> inspiratoryPhase captures the requirement, to be enforced globally, i.e., for every breath cycle in PSV mode.

The natural language requirement states that when PSV Mode is active, each breath cycle shall begin with the inspiration phase. This means that whenever PSV mode is selected, it is required that the start of a breath cycle coincides with the start of the inspiration phase. 

The most accurate atomic propositions for these events are PSVMode (for PSV mode active), and inspiratoryPhaseStart (for the inspiratory phase of the breath cycle starting). The correct LTL template is a safety template that expresses: 'Globally, if PSVMode holds, then inspiratoryPhaseStart must hold.' This ensures that whenever the system is in PSV mode, every new breath cycle starts with the inspiration phase, as required by the input statement.

Thus, we use LTL template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), set bool_exp1 to PSVMode, and bool_exp3 to inspiratoryPhaseStart. This directly captures the stated requirement. N_DURATION and the other boolean expressions are not required for this template, and are left blank.
